entry_text:
Item #: SCP-007 | Object Class: Euclid
Recovered from a rent-stabilized apartment where every window showed the same starless night, SCP-007 now occupies a lightless vault monitored by analog instruments only. Entry requires continuous name-calls at sixty-second intervals; any response from an unlisted voice triggers immediate evacuation and salt-line reseal.
SCP-007 is a negative space that remembers what has been removed: absent objects continue to cast shadows, fog glass, and speak in the gaps where they once stood. These omissions learn the room over time, arranging furniture to accommodate their invisible bodies and asking, politely, to be let back in.
Scene Prompt 1: A chair’s shadow sits occupied, though the chair itself is missing
Scene Prompt 2: Dust handwriting curves around an invisible handprint on black glass
